title: polypodium vulgare f. commune
intro: Polypodium vulgare f. commune, also known as common polypody, is a fern species that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of common polypody include its ability to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Polypodium vulgare f. commune include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
